case.focal_claim: … finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs [compared to villages dominated by upper caste].

original_datasets.name: household
original_datasets.columns: [hhcode, village, caste, totinc, cropinc, cropincacre, domlow, domhigh (in table2.dta), literate, totland, pricland, buywater, ownpump]

summary_statistics.info: 1666 rows, 85 columns; key types include category(5), float32(66), float64(10), int16(2), int32(2). Non-missing for domlow: 1295.

original_datasets.notes: Dataset includes household-level total income (totinc) and village dominance indicator (domlow). The 'caste' variable in this file contains lower-caste categories; the reanalysis t-test compares income by village dominance.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Compare total household income between low-caste dominated and upper-caste dominated villages using an independent-samples t-test.
preprocessing_steps: Drop rows with missing values in these variables.
Form two groups by domlow (1 vs 0).

key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop observations with missing totinc or domlow.
key_choices.data_processing: None beyond basic filtering for non-missing fields.
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold (e.g., p<0.05) for the difference in means.

analysis_code.notes: Script reads /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data/household.dta, performs Welch t-test of totinc by domlow, and writes results to /app/data/outputs/results_12hio.txt.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Do not use District controls, Crop controls, Distance controls, Groundwater controls, Public goods controls. Do not include Irrigation, credit, and tenancy variables, and their interaction term versions. Do not use instruments for water buyer status.

analysis_path.path_description: Using no controls or instruments, compare total household income between low-caste dominated and upper-caste dominated villages using an independent-samples t-test.
preprocessing_steps: Drop rows with missing values in these variables.
Form two groups by domlow (1 vs 0).

key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop observations with missing totinc or domlow.
key_choices.data_processing: None beyond basic filtering for non-missing fields.
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold (e.g., p<0.05) for the difference in means.

analysis_code.notes: Same script as Task1; executes the no-controls t-test consistent with the Task2 instruction.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned no-controls t-test path. Do not replace it with a regression or an IV model.

files.data/analysis_12hio_tasks__py.py: Python entry script. Loads /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data/household.dta, performs Welch t-test of totinc by domlow, and writes results to /app/data/outputs/results_12hio.txt.

codebase.notes: Original Stata program.txt documents multiple tables, but the proposed analysis uses t-tests on income by village dominance without controls. All execution is translated to a single Python script for containerized runs.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset and produce structured outputs (t-statistic, p-value, group means, and conclusion) to /app/data/outputs/results_12hio.txt.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: t_statistic, p_value, mean_difference, group_means, N1, N0, conclusion_text
